title: centaurea glaberrima subsp. divergens
intro: Centaurea glaberrima subsp. divergens, also known as the prickly knapweed or divergent knapweed, is a plant species within the Centaurea genus, characterized by its distinctive prickly leaves and purple flowers. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues, such as diarrhea and gastritis, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Centaurea glaberrima subsp. divergens include its potential to soothe digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antiseptic properties, making it a valuable herb for maintaining overall well-being. Herbal preparations of Centaurea glaberrima subsp. divergens often include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote health and vitality.
